AP scores with big FEC project

Brant Houston NICAR Managing Director December 1994
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsGovernment & Politics

An AP team led by Bill Dedman and Richard Mullins used computer-assisted reporting and FEC data to create 25 detailed reports on campaign contributions for 1,300 federal candidates before the November elections. The project pioneered the use of database management to analyze donor patterns and connect congressional actions to funding sources.

An Associated Press project led by Bill Dedman and Richard Mullins showcased the power of computer-assisted reporting ahead of the November federal elections. By creatively using data from the Federal Election Commission, the team distributed over 25 detailed reports to AP bureaus nationwide covering contribution data for 1,300 federal candidates. This groundbreaking effort utilized FoxPro database manager to handle extensive data sets and convert them into accessible charts that classified donor information by categories like geography and special interests.

This project not only boosted election coverage but also laid the groundwork for scrutinizing congressional actions against funding sources.
